q: Centripetal force is the force acting towards the centre of the circular path.
Centrifugal force is a real force acting on an object along the string.
Centrifugal force is an apparent or fictitious force equal and opposite to the centripetal force.
The centrifugal force is caused by the effect of inertia.
In a laboratory centrifuge, denser particles rise to the top of the tube.
The sedimentation principle states that radial acceleration causes denser particles to settle to the bottom.
A centrifugal pump converts mechanical energy into hydraulic energy.
Hydraulic energy is also known as pressure energy.
The centrifugal action of a washing machine is used to wash the clothes with soap.
Centrifugal governors consist of two balls of equal mass attached to arms.
The centrifugal governor is used to measure and regulate the speed of a machine.
A satellite orbiting a planet is supported by the centrifugal force.
Removing excess water by spin drying clothes is done with the help of centrifugal force.
Centrifugal force results from the effect of inertia.
Centripetal force is directed towards the axis of rotation or centre of curvature.
